audit: cor:grand sharper (q>=2n) 86-bit branch: row-by-row hypothesis check on the deployed prize envelope

follows_up_on.flagged_observation: Observation, not a correction: cor:grand's last sentence gives a sharper conditional number 2^-42 (86 bits above target) if q>=2n, worth a future footnote.

hypotheses.H5_domain_type: D <= B^x is a multiplicative coset of order n (thm:main's own hypothesis; a circle/twin-coset chi-image domain is (T_a,a)-smooth, not (X^a,a)-smooth, hence not an instance of this hypothesis -- proved via the separate thm:phi-cap, not thm:main)
hypotheses.H7_sharper_branch_extra: q >= 2n (cor:grand's own extra hypothesis for the improved >=2^-42 conclusion; only meaningful once H1-H6/H5 already hold, since the corollary being instantiated is cor:grand itself)

worst_case_integer_inequality.description: generic conditional floor at envelope-worst k=2^40: 1/(4k) = 2^-42 exactly (4*2^40 = 2^42)

kb_sextic.title: KoalaBear sextic (cor:deployed, s=1 top of the cor:rows interleaved family)
kb_sextic.corollary_that_certifies_this_row_in_the: cor:deployed (direct application of thm:main with N=256, not routed through cor:grand)
kb_sextic.domain_type: genuine multiplicative subgroup of B^x = F_p^x (order n)

row_own_printed_bound.exact_tight_value_is_essentially: just under 2^-21 (~107 bits above target); cor:deployed rounds down to the clean >2^-22 headline

kb_sextic.verdict: cor:grand's sharper branch is a true, fully verified statement about this row (all of H1-H7 hold by exact integer check), but it is scientifically redundant here: cor:deployed already prints a bound (2^-22, 106 bits above target) that is 20 bits better than the generic worst-case conditional floor (2^-42, 86 bits) this branch would contribute, and that bound needs no q>=2n hypothesis at all.

m31_circle_line_round.title: Mersenne-31/QM31 circle line round (cor:circle-deployed(a))
m31_circle_line_round.domain_type: D = chi(twin coset), (T_a,a)-Chebyshev-smooth -- NOT a multiplicative coset of B^x

hyp_H2_Nrho_divides_n.note: size hypothesis alone would pass; irrelevant because H5 already fails

hyp_H7_q_ge_2n.note: numerically true but moot: H5 already blocks cor:grand

m31_circle_line_round.target_note: q < 2^128, so delta*_C(2^-128) is degenerate (prop:small-field); the paper's own meaningful target for this row is 2^-100, not 2^-128
m31_circle_line_round.verdict: cor:grand's sharper branch (and its base branch) do not apply: the circle line-round domain is not a multiplicative coset, so it is not an instance of thm:main/cor:grand at all -- it is certified by the separate, more general thm:phi-cap route (cor:circle-grand/cor:circle-deployed), which does not print an analogous q>=2n sharpening. Discharging this would require a new (unprinted) sharpening lemma for thm:phi-cap; not attempted here.

m31_circle_code.title: Mersenne-31/QM31 bivariate circle code (cor:circle-deployed(b))
m31_circle_code.corollary_that_certifies_this_row_in_the: cor:circle-deployed(b), via thm:phi-cap on the torus-uniformized code (NOT thm:main / cor:grand)
m31_circle_code.domain_type: torus twin coset (union of 2 cosets, not a single coset) with a_scale always even, so a does not divide k_c -- ever
hyp_H5_failure_reasons: the twin coset is a union of two cosets gH and g^-1H, not itself a single multiplicative coset

thm:main additionally needs a dividing k for its integer scale a=n/N; on this row's dyadic torus twin coset every usable scale a is even while k_c=2w+1 is always odd, so a never divides k_c (printed remark near tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4010)

hyp_H7_q_ge_2n.note: numerically true but moot: H5 already blocks cor:grand

m31_circle_code.target_note: same q < 2^128 degeneracy as the line-round row; meaningful target is 2^-100
m31_circle_code.verdict: Same non-applicability as the line-round row, plus a second, structural (parity) obstruction unique to the circle-code uniformization: k_c is always odd, so thm:main's a|k hypothesis can never hold here even in principle. Not a gap to discharge -- a hard mismatch between this row's object and cor:grand's mechanism.

f17_32_cycle116_119.title: F_17^32, n=512, k=256 (Cycle116/119 proof-record rows)
f17_32_cycle116_119.corollary_that_certifies_this_row_in_the: not a corollary of this paper's pigeonhole machinery at all -- a direct finite point-count (imports the Cycle84 finite count; see PR #736 note part 1(b))
f17_32_cycle116_119.B_natural_reading: F itself (no smaller subfield used in the construction; domainKind = multiplicative-subgroup per site/data/rate-leaderboards.json)
f17_32_cycle116_119.domain_type: genuine multiplicative subgroup (domainKind: multiplicative-subgroup) -- same TYPE as the KB row, unlike the circle rows

hyp_H7_q_ge_2n.note: numerically true by an enormous margin but moot: H2 already blocks cor:grand

row_own_printed_bound.source: Cycle116/Cycle119 finite point-count (site/data/rate-leaderboards.json ids cycle116-rho-1-2, cycle119-rho-1-2)
row_own_printed_bound.note: less than even the generic UNCONDITIONAL cor:grand floor (42 bits); consistent with cor:grand simply not covering this row (H2 fails), so no contradiction

diagnostic_N256_eq_hyp_check.description: Exploratory, not part of cor:grand's own hypothesis list (which fixes N=N_rho=1024): checks whether thm:main's own entropy hypothesis eq:hyp holds for this row at N=256 (the smaller N that rescues the KB interleaved family in cor:rows), with B=F (this row's construction has no smaller subfield to exploit, unlike KB/M31).
diagnostic_N256_eq_hyp_check.interpretation: fails by about 2 bits at this one candidate N; not an exhaustive search over all valid N, so this is evidence (not proof) that the row's flat-field structure (B=F, no subfield gap) makes it a genuinely harder regime for this paper's pigeonhole method, not a one-clause technicality

f17_32_cycle116_119.verdict: cor:grand does not apply at all (blocked at H2, independent of q vs 2n, which holds trivially/vacuously). The row's own best number (32.82 bits) sits below even the generic unconditional 42-bit floor, with no contradiction since the hypothesis genuinely fails here.

interleaved_kb_family_boundary_diagnosti.description: cor:rows interleaves C_s = RS[F_p^6, D_s, k_s] for s=2^j, 0<=j<=12, n_s=2^21/s. Checking cor:grand's own N_rho=1024 divisibility hypothesis (not cor:rows' own N=256 choice) against n_s for each s.

j_12_s_4096.note: same n=512 boundary failure as the F_17^32 row -- moot for cor:rows itself, which uses N=256 (not N_rho=1024) and is unaffected

verdict_summary.headline: q>=2n holds trivially on all four anchor rows (by construction, huge margins). But cor:grand's full hypothesis set -- the point of this audit -- is satisfied by exactly one of the four (KB sextic); the other three are certified by structurally different corollaries (cor:circle-grand/deployed via thm:phi-cap for the two M31 rows; a direct finite count for F_17^32) that are not instances of cor:grand. And on the one row where the sharper branch does apply, it is dominated by an already-printed, already-unconditional row-specific bound that is 20 bits better. Net: citing cor:grand's 86-bit conditional branch adds no new pay-per-bit value on the deployed envelope as currently listed.
